nodes.title: POC in Germany and Austria
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: POC in Germany and Austria
nodes.description: Examination of the experiences of People of Color in Germany and Austria reflected in autobiographical texts by Black children growing up in the Third Reich, the children of transnational marriage, the children of African American GIs and German/Austrian women, as well as in contemporary texts and films. Prerequisites: GERM 2200 or GERM 2202.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Elementary German I
nodes.description: An introduction to all aspects of contemporary standard German: Speaking, listening, reading, writing. Cultural components include topics such as: music, art, literature, and current events.

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Intermediate German I
nodes.description: Comprehensive review of German grammar, vocabulary-building skills, development of reading strategies and compositional abilities, study of contemporary German culture through literary texts. Prerequisite: GERM 1200 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Linguistics of the German Wrld
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Elementary German II
nodes.description: An introduction to all aspects of contemporary standard German: Speaking, listening, reading, writing. Cultural components include topics such as: music, art, literature, and current events. Prerequisite: GERM 1100 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereq enforced by the system: Hybrid course: fourth meeting online; Open to Degree and PACE students * This course fulfills the requirements for both GC2: Developing Global Citizens requirement; and OC: Oral Communication.

nodes.title: Interm Germ II: Sustainability
nodes.description: Enhancement of language proficiency with focus on effective, contextually relevant communication; emphasis on personalized content, learning strategies, and reflection skills; exploration of a variety of current issues and standpoints in contemporary German speaking countries with an emphasis on sustainability. Students may take 1 German course numbered between 2200 and 2209 for credit. Prerequisite: GERM 2100 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override
